I'm
very well.

Um So I'm on my work, my like desktop phone, but then I have you up on my computer.
Ok,
great.
And I only ask because I wanted to show you a couple of things today.

See if any of those are appealing to you, we could talk about potentially what it would look like to get those added or if that's not interesting, we just keep it as usual and um we keep moving along with the plans that we have, which presently are to train your two other property managers.

K?

Not at this time.
Terrific.

Yes, I think it is.
I think some of them utilize it.
I don't think all of them do necessarily.
Um,
so, yeah, and then I think just the other thing is that, um, like I'm young, I know how to use, use it.
I just feel like some of maybe our older property managers just aren't comfortable doing it.

not annoying but annoying to me.
But um yeah, other than that, everything's great.
I get it.

So it could be something as simple as that or it could be, we have some less tech savvy folks who are a little, um, kind of standoffish with the tool.
Um So happy to talk about that as well if that's, if that's something helpful for us.
Um, but I do like to see that there's some consistent usage in there in a, in a core group.
So, so that makes me feel better uh about what's going on there.
Um I've got a quick little demonstration about our road map that I'd love to share with you.
I don't believe I've shared with you our road map yet.
Uh Because as, as my understanding, this is probably the first time we've kind of sat down together and had a bit of a state of the union.
Is that correct?
Right.
So, um it's wonderful to meet you and I promise I'll keep this brief.
I won't shut your ear off for the whole half hour.
Um But just wanted to let you know what we're up to.
Uh things have changed quite a bit even since you guys signed your renewal.
Uh Last January, we've added three key features that are listed here.

One that is about to come out, that is the market survey tab, which I think could help when I hear that folks kind of downstream on the on the asset management or property management community manager side aren't using the tool as much.
We developed this market survey tab to speak directly to those users.
So I'll show you that in a bit.
But key things, we've launched a daily unit sync
for those organizations that have some data warehousing tools like Snowflake, dropbox, Excel, Google Sheets, power B I.
Anybody who's kind of using that as a financial modeling tool or a forecasting tool.
We can now push um apartment IQ data every day automatically into those tools.
So that's been really helpful for folks on that side of the house, even rev operations managers.
So um not sure if that's something that appeals to you, but something that we did launch to speak to that camp of customers,
we also launched concessions last quarter and, and this is kind of a, a revamp of concessions.
We're not just pulling a number, we're pulling the details because a concession isn't always a flat number listed on a website.
We can pull this exact text that's coming through.
If it's a value that applies to rent, we can also list that rent
and then because we have that rent number, I can give you an N er calculation as well for all your units at the unit specific level even.
So that's been really helpful for a lot of customers uh because it just removes a step from the calculation.
Right.
Right.
Now what that looks like in the platform, I'm gonna go ahead and open up our um IQ property IQ Management account.
Um What that looks like at first this is the page that you're probably already pretty familiar with, but you'll notice now that we do have a couple of new columns, right?

So we have an average concession value, an average n er a concession detail column that highlights if there's any uh it, it shows how many specific concessions are going in the market right uh across your properties.

where you'll see this additional concession value here
and all the way down to the units level
where you'll see the concession details in n er for each specific apartment, each specific unit across the market survey.

it is not, it is not.
So we'll talk about that.
I don't wanna actually let me just be very upfront with you.

This is kind of our benchmark, but for this for you, I believe that would be a $50 increase per property and we're not trying to, you know, ruin anybody's budgeting, especially this time of year.
Uh So we're willing to work with you here.
I think you're on 100 $50 per property right now.

So 199 is where new folks come in.
That's not where I expect PK management to come in.
Got you.

Um I was alluding to it up here on this slide.

This market survey tab that we've launched is our attempt to create a perspective or a view that is a lot more familiar to those on-site folks who are in the trenches every day trying to put together a market survey.
You and your operations related teams will still have access to the regular property tab, right?
That's not going anywhere, unit type units.
It's not going anywhere, but we'll add this market survey tab, which is now going to highlight things in a more familiar layout for a lot of property managers.
And it's also going to add a couple of metrics that we've been really excited to bring in.
Most notably, a leasing velocity setup where we'll have leases rolling the last 30 days, leases rolling the last seven days and then an average of leases per week
over a four week period.
And then we also do the same for availability um so that we can kind of develop an absorption rate and that you and your team can develop an absorption, right?
So you can see in the last seven days, Rebecca urban least one, right, in the last three days, at least two.

So, are we replacing quicker for availability, then we're playing it off
and then that concession detail is still live here.

What are your first thoughts here?
Um I mean, it's pretty user friendly, but then again, I'm speaking for myself.
Um
yeah,
but I mean, it seems pretty self explanatory once you get in there.
